encounter.reason: Bilateral hand stiffness and swelling, rheumatology workup
encounter.location: Eastbay Primary Care Associates, Oakland, CA

screening.name: PHQ-2
screening.interpretation: No significant depressive symptoms; negative screen for depression

conditions.name: seropositive rheumatoid arthritis
conditions.description: Inflammatory polyarthritis affecting bilateral metacarpophalangeal (MCP) and proximal interphalangeal (PIP) joints, more severe on the right hand. Characterized by morning stiffness lasting approximately 90 minutes, pain with joint movement, reduced grip strength, and mild soft-tissue swelling on examination.

medications.name: acetaminophen
medications.reason: inflammatory arthritis pain associated with seropositive rheumatoid arthritis

document_references.title: Office Visit Progress Note
document_references.description: Comprehensive documentation of 2023-03-17 office visit including detailed history of 2–3 months of progressive bilateral hand and wrist pain with morning stiffness and functional limitation (difficulty opening jars); physical examination findings including bilateral MCP and PIP joint tenderness with mild soft-tissue swelling (worse on right), reduced grip strength, and normal skin/nails; complete vital signs (BP, HR, temperature, RR, SpO₂, weight, BMI) and PHQ-2 screening; laboratory results with interpretation (positive RF, positive anti-CCP, elevated inflammatory markers); clinical assessment of inflammatory polyarthritis highly suspicious for seropositive rheumatoid arthritis based on clinical presentation and serology; treatment plan including acetaminophen recommendation, NSAID caution, and expedited rheumatology referral with detailed rationale for urgent specialist evaluation; discussion of results with patient and return precautions for worsening pain, swelling, or new systemic symptoms.

diagnostic_reports.description: Unremarkable; no acute hematologic abnormality identified.
Unremarkable; no abnormality in renal function, liver function, or electrolytes.
